questions.question: Briefly explain the role of an 'actuator' in an IoT system. (2 marks)
expectedPoints: An actuator is a component that performs a physical action.
It converts an electrical signal (output from processing) into a physical motion or control action.
Examples include turning on a motor, opening a valve, or sounding an alarm.

questions.question: Describe the logical design of a Level-4 IoT system. Use a smart home lighting system as an example. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: Level-4 involves multiple sensing nodes and a gateway.
Description of components: Multiple smart light bulbs (sensors/actuators) and light switches (sensors).
Role of Gateway: A central hub (gateway) that collects commands from switches and controls the bulbs, often using a protocol like Zigbee for local communication.
Local Processing: The gateway may process rules locally (e.g., 'if evening, turn on porch light').
Cloud Storage: The gateway also connects to the cloud to store usage data and receive firmware updates or remote commands from a user's phone app.
